title: Authors and Citation
text: Ricardo Oliveros-Ramos. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Oliveros-Ramos R (2026). nctools: Tools Manipulating netCDF Files. R package version 0.2, https://roliveros-ramos.github.io/nctools.
code: @Manual{,   title = {nctools: Tools for Manipulating netCDF Files},   author = {Ricardo Oliveros-Ramos},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2},   url = {https://roliveros-ramos.github.io/nctools}, }

what: Tools for Writing and Manipulating netCDF Files
title: Tools for Manipulating netCDF Files
text: core, nctools designed make easy write array data R netCDF files, inspect manipulate files lightweight helper functions. R package, nctools, provides helper functions creating, editing, subsetting, concatenating, transforming netCDF files within R. main entry point write_ncdf(), offers quick convenient way write array data generated R netCDF files minimal boilerplate. Built around ncdf4, package also includes utilities inspecting variables dimensions, reading writing attributes, renaming variables dimensions, subsetting variables dimension values, concatenating records along unlimited dimensions, changing longitude conventions, applying summary functions selected margins. package especially useful modelling environmental workflows arrays first generated processed R need exported netCDF format storage, exchange, downstream analysis. See https://roliveros-ramos.github.io/nctools/ documentation examples.

title: Tools for Manipulating netCDF Files
text: Note: nctools depends ncdf4, installation may require working system netCDF library.
code: # Once available on CRAN: install.packages("nctools")  # Development version from GitHub: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("roliveros-ramos/nctools")

title: Tools for Manipulating netCDF Files
text: typical workflow nctools starts array data already available R. function write_ncdf() provides simple interface turn arrays netCDF files defining variable name, dimensions, metadata directly R. Load package:
code: library(nctools)

what: Quick start: write an array to a netCDF file
title: Tools for Manipulating netCDF Files
text: main entry point package write_ncdf(), makes easy export array data created R netCDF file. creates netCDF file directly R array, named dimensions basic metadata, single step. netCDF file created, nctools provides set utilities common follow-tasks. Subset variable using dimension bounds: Compute summary statistic selected margins: Change longitude convention variable: Useful entry points explore package include:
code: x <- array(rnorm(24), dim = c(4, 3, 2))  write_ncdf(   x,   filename = "example.nc",   varid = "temp",   dim = list(     lon = seq(-72, -69, length.out = 4),     lat = seq(-20, -18, length.out = 3),     depth = c(0, 10)   ),   longname = "Temperature",   units = "degree_C" ) nc_subset(   filename = "example.nc",   varid = "temp",   output = "subset.nc",   lon = c(-71, -69),   depth = 10 ) nc_mean(   filename = "example.nc",   varid = "temp",   MARGIN = c("lon", "lat"),   output = "temp_mean.nc" ) nc_changePrimeMeridian(   filename = "example.nc",   output = "example_180.nc",   varid = "temp",   MARGIN = "lon",   primeMeridian = "center" ) ?write_ncdf ?nc_subset ?nc_apply ?nc_changePrimeMeridian

title: Tools for Manipulating netCDF Files
text: find bug, questions documentation, want suggest enhancement, please open issue. Contributions welcome pull requests.

what: Standardise longitude values to a selected prime meridian convention — checkLongitude
title: Standardise longitude values to a selected prime meridian convention — checkLongitude
text: Converts longitude values either `[-180, 180]` convention `[0, 360]` convention.

title: Standardise longitude values to a selected prime meridian convention — checkLongitude
code: checkLongitude(x, primeMeridian = "center", sort = FALSE, ...)

title: Standardise longitude values to a selected prime meridian convention — checkLongitude
text: x Numeric vector longitude values. primeMeridian Character string specifying target longitude convention. Use `"center"` longitudes `[-180, 180]` range `"left"` longitudes `[0, 360]` range. sort Logical. `TRUE`, sort output values conversion. ... Additional arguments. Currently unused.

title: Standardise longitude values to a selected prime meridian convention — checkLongitude
text: numeric vector longitude values expressed requested   convention.

title: Standardise longitude values to a selected prime meridian convention — checkLongitude
text: Longitude values modified needed. `primeMeridian = "center"`, values greater `180` shifted subtracting `360`. `primeMeridian = "left"`, negative values shifted adding `360`.

title: Standardise longitude values to a selected prime meridian convention — checkLongitude
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ lon <- c(170, 180, 190, 350)  checkLongitude(lon, primeMeridian = "center") checkLongitude(lon, primeMeridian = "left") } # }

what: Identify the prime meridian convention from longitude values — findPrimeMeridian
title: Identify the prime meridian convention from longitude values — findPrimeMeridian
text: Infers whether longitude vector expressed using `[-180, 180]` convention `[0, 360]` convention.

title: Identify the prime meridian convention from longitude values — findPrimeMeridian
code: findPrimeMeridian(x)

title: Identify the prime meridian convention from longitude values — findPrimeMeridian
text: x Numeric vector longitude values.

title: Identify the prime meridian convention from longitude values — findPrimeMeridian
text: character string, either `"center"` `"left"`, `NULL`   convention determined.

title: Identify the prime meridian convention from longitude values — findPrimeMeridian
text: function returns `"center"` longitude negative, `"left"` longitude greater `180`. neither condition met, convention determined unambiguously function returns `NULL` warning.

title: Identify the prime meridian convention from longitude values — findPrimeMeridian
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ findPrimeMeridian(c(-10, 0, 20)) findPrimeMeridian(c(10, 180, 350)) } # }

what: Apply a function over margins of a netCDF variable — nc_apply
title: Apply a function over margins of a netCDF variable — nc_apply
text: Reads variable netCDF file, applies function one dimensions using [base::apply()], writes result new netCDF file.

title: Apply a function over margins of a netCDF variable — nc_apply
code: nc_apply(   filename,   varid,   MARGIN,   FUN,   ...,   output = NULL,   drop = FALSE,   newdim = NULL,   name = NULL,   longname = NULL,   units = NULL,   compression = NA,   verbose = FALSE,   force_v4 = TRUE,   ignore.case = FALSE )

title: Apply a function over margins of a netCDF variable — nc_apply
text: filename Character string giving path input netCDF file. varid Character string giving name variable process. missing file contains single variable, variable used. MARGIN Integer character vector specifying dimensions retain output, [base::apply()]. Dimension names may used. FUN Function apply. ... Additional arguments passed `FUN`. output Character string giving path output netCDF file create. drop Logical. Currently implemented. newdim Optional numeric values dimension created `FUN` returns vector length greater one. name Optional character string giving name output variable. default, original variable name used. longname Optional character string giving long name output variable. default, name based `FUN` original variable long name generated. units Optional character string giving units output variable. default, units input variable used. compression Optional numeric compression level output variable. verbose Logical. Currently unused. force_v4 Logical. Currently unused internally. ignore.case Logical. `TRUE`, ignore case matching dimension names supplied `MARGIN`.

title: Apply a function over margins of a netCDF variable — nc_apply
text: Invisibly returns `output`.

title: Apply a function over margins of a netCDF variable — nc_apply
text: selected variable read memory processed [base::apply()]. output retains dimensions specified `MARGIN`. `FUN` returns vector length greater one, additional dimension appended result; coordinate values taken `newdim` provided, generated automatically otherwise.

title: Apply a function over margins of a netCDF variable — nc_apply
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Mean over the time dimension nc_apply(   filename = "input.nc",   varid = "temp",   MARGIN = c("lon", "lat"),   FUN = mean,   na.rm = TRUE,   output = "temp_mean.nc" )  ## Quantiles over the depth dimension nc_apply(   filename = "input.nc",   varid = "temp",   MARGIN = c("lon", "lat"),   FUN = quantile,   probs = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75),   newdim = c(25, 50, 75),   output = "temp_quantiles.nc" ) } # }

what: Change the prime meridian of a variable in a netCDF file — nc_changePrimeMeridian
title: Change the prime meridian of a variable in a netCDF file — nc_changePrimeMeridian
text: Reorders longitude dimension variable longitudes follow either `[-180, 180]` convention `[0, 360]` convention, writes result netCDF file.

title: Change the prime meridian of a variable in a netCDF file — nc_changePrimeMeridian
code: nc_changePrimeMeridian(   filename,   output,   varid = NA,   MARGIN = 1,   primeMeridian = "center",   verbose = FALSE,   overwrite = FALSE,   compression = NA,   mem.limit = 3072,   ignore.case = FALSE )

title: Change the prime meridian of a variable in a netCDF file — nc_changePrimeMeridian
text: filename Character string giving path input netCDF file. output Optional character string giving path output netCDF file. omitted, `overwrite = TRUE` must used input file replaced. varid Variable identifier. can character string giving variable name. omitted file contains single variable, variable used. MARGIN Integer character string length one specifying longitude dimension. can either dimension index name. primeMeridian Character string specifying target longitude convention. Use `"center"` longitudes `[-180, 180]` range `"left"` longitudes `[0, 360]` range. verbose Logical. `TRUE`, report progress processing variable. overwrite Logical. `TRUE`, allow overwriting existing output file, including input file . compression Optional numeric compression level output variable. Supplying typically requires netCDF4 output. mem.limit Numeric. Approximate memory limit, MiB, used processing large variables. variable exceeds limit, reordered iteratively chunks. ignore.case Logical. `TRUE`, ignore case matching `varid` character `MARGIN`.

title: Change the prime meridian of a variable in a netCDF file — nc_changePrimeMeridian
text: Invisibly returns `output`.

title: Change the prime meridian of a variable in a netCDF file — nc_changePrimeMeridian
text: function updates longitude coordinate values selected variable, reorders data accordingly, writes result new netCDF file. variable small enough, full array processed memory. Otherwise, variable processed iteratively chunks reduce memory use. longitude values already follow requested convention, function returns warning. `output` differs `filename`, input file copied `output` unchanged.

title: Change the prime meridian of a variable in a netCDF file — nc_changePrimeMeridian
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ nc_changePrimeMeridian(   filename = "input.nc",   output = "output.nc",   varid = "temp",   MARGIN = "lon",   primeMeridian = "center" )  nc_changePrimeMeridian(   filename = "input.nc",   output = "output_pacific.nc",   varid = "temp",   MARGIN = 1,   primeMeridian = "left",   compression = 4 ) } # }

title: Extract a variable to a new netCDF file — nc_extract
code: nc_extract(filename, varid, output)

title: Extract a variable to a new netCDF file — nc_extract
text: filename Character string giving path input netCDF file. varid Character string giving name variable extract. missing file contains single variable, variable used. output Character string giving path output netCDF file create.

title: Extract a variable to a new netCDF file — nc_extract
text: Invisibly returns `output`.

title: Extract a variable to a new netCDF file — nc_extract
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ nc_extract(   filename = "input.nc",   varid = "temp",   output = "temp_only.nc" ) } # }

what: Locate the depth where a variable reaches a given value — nc_loc
title: Locate the depth where a variable reaches a given value — nc_loc
text: Finds position along one dimension netCDF variable variable reaches target value, writes result new netCDF file. typical use case, used estimate depth variable equals `loc`, using linear interpolation adjacent depth levels needed.

title: Locate the depth where a variable reaches a given value — nc_loc
code: nc_loc(   filename,   varid,   MARGIN,   loc,   output = NULL,   drop = TRUE,   newdim = NULL,   name = NULL,   longname = NULL,   units = NULL,   compression = NA,   verbose = FALSE,   force_v4 = TRUE,   ignore.case = FALSE )

title: Locate the depth where a variable reaches a given value — nc_loc
text: filename Character string. Path input netCDF file. varid Character string. Name variable analyse. missing file contains one variable, variable used. MARGIN Integer character string length one. Dimension along target value searched. can either dimension index name (example `"depth"`). loc Numeric. Target value locate variable. output Character string. Path output netCDF file create. drop Logical. degenerate dimensions dropped? Currently used internally. newdim Reserved future use. Currently used internally. name Character string. Name output variable. default, name searched dimension used. longname Character string. Long name output variable. default, descriptive name generated automatically. units Character string. Units output variable. Currently used internally; default, units searched dimension used. compression Numeric. Compression level output variable. `NA`, compression applied netCDF4 output required. verbose Logical. extra information printed? Currently used internally. force_v4 Logical. output file forced netCDF4? Currently used internally. ignore.case Logical. Ignore case matching dimension name `MARGIN`?

title: Locate the depth where a variable reaches a given value — nc_loc
text: Invisibly returns path created output file.

title: Locate the depth where a variable reaches a given value — nc_loc
text: function searches first sign change `variable - loc` along selected dimension, estimates exact position linear interpolation two surrounding grid points. output file contains new variable searched dimension removed. example, `MARGIN` corresponds depth, output field giving estimated depth `varid` reaches `loc` remaining combination dimensions.

title: Locate the depth where a variable reaches a given value — nc_loc
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Estimate the depth of the 20 degree isotherm nc_loc(   filename = "temperature.nc",   varid = "temp",   MARGIN = "depth",   loc = 20,   output = "depth_20C.nc" ) } # }

what: Compute summary statistics of a netCDF variable — nc_mean
title: Compute summary statistics of a netCDF variable — nc_mean
text: Applies summary function variable netCDF file dimensions listed `MARGIN`, writes result new netCDF file.

title: Compute summary statistics of a netCDF variable — nc_mean
code: nc_mean(   filename,   varid,   MARGIN = c(1, 2),   na.rm = TRUE,   trim = 0,   output = NULL,   drop = TRUE,   compression = NA,   verbose = FALSE,   force_v4 = TRUE,   ignore.case = FALSE )  nc_min(   filename,   varid,   MARGIN = c(1, 2),   na.rm = TRUE,   output = NULL,   drop = TRUE,   compression = NA,   verbose = FALSE,   force_v4 = TRUE,   ignore.case = FALSE )  nc_max(   filename,   varid,   MARGIN = c(1, 2),   na.rm = TRUE,   output = NULL,   drop = TRUE,   compression = NA,   verbose = FALSE,   force_v4 = TRUE,   ignore.case = FALSE )

title: Compute summary statistics of a netCDF variable — nc_mean
text: filename Character string giving path input netCDF file. varid Character string giving name variable process. missing file contains single variable, variable used. MARGIN Integer character vector specifying dimensions retain output, [base::apply()]. Dimension names may used. na.rm Logical. `TRUE`, missing values removed computing statistic. trim Numeric scalar giving fraction observations trimmed end computing mean. Passed [base::mean()]. Used `nc_mean()`. output Character string giving path output netCDF file create. drop Logical. Currently implemented. compression Optional numeric compression level output variable. verbose Logical. Currently unused. force_v4 Logical. Currently unused internally. ignore.case Logical. `TRUE`, ignore case matching dimension names supplied `MARGIN`.

title: Compute summary statistics of a netCDF variable — nc_mean
text: Invisibly returns `output`.

title: Compute summary statistics of a netCDF variable — nc_mean
text: `nc_mean()` applies [base::mean()], `nc_min()` applies [base::min()], `nc_max()` applies [base::max()]. output retains dimensions specified `MARGIN`.

title: Compute summary statistics of a netCDF variable — nc_mean
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ nc_mean(   filename = "input.nc",   varid = "temp",   MARGIN = c("lon", "lat"),   na.rm = TRUE,   output = "temp_mean.nc" )  nc_min(   filename = "input.nc",   varid = "temp",   MARGIN = c("lon", "lat"),   na.rm = TRUE,   output = "temp_min.nc" )  nc_max(   filename = "input.nc",   varid = "temp",   MARGIN = c("lon", "lat"),   na.rm = TRUE,   output = "temp_max.nc" ) } # }

what: Compute sample quantiles of a netCDF variable — nc_quantile
title: Compute sample quantiles of a netCDF variable — nc_quantile

title: Compute sample quantiles of a netCDF variable — nc_quantile
code: nc_quantile(   filename,   varid,   MARGIN = c(1, 2),   na.rm = TRUE,   probs = c(0, 0.5, 1),   output = NULL,   drop = TRUE,   compression = NA,   verbose = FALSE,   force_v4 = TRUE,   ignore.case = FALSE )

title: Compute sample quantiles of a netCDF variable — nc_quantile
text: filename Character string giving path input netCDF file. varid Character string giving name variable process. missing file contains single variable, variable used. MARGIN Integer character vector specifying dimensions retain output, [base::apply()]. Dimension names may used. na.rm Logical. `TRUE`, missing values removed computing quantiles. probs Numeric vector probabilities `[0, 1]` passed [stats::quantile()]. output Character string giving path output netCDF file create. drop Logical. Currently implemented. compression Optional numeric compression level output variable. verbose Logical. Currently unused. force_v4 Logical. Currently unused internally. ignore.case Logical. `TRUE`, ignore case matching dimension names supplied `MARGIN`.

title: Compute sample quantiles of a netCDF variable — nc_quantile
text: Invisibly returns `output`.

title: Compute sample quantiles of a netCDF variable — nc_quantile
text: output retains dimensions specified `MARGIN`. additional dimension appended store quantiles defined `probs`.

title: Compute sample quantiles of a netCDF variable — nc_quantile
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ nc_quantile(   filename = "input.nc",   varid = "temp",   MARGIN = c("lon", "lat"),   probs = c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9),   output = "temp_quantiles.nc" ) } # }

what: Concatenate records of a variable across netCDF files — nc_rcat
title: Concatenate records of a variable across netCDF files — nc_rcat

title: Concatenate records of a variable across netCDF files — nc_rcat
code: nc_rcat(filenames, varid, output)

title: Concatenate records of a variable across netCDF files — nc_rcat
text: filenames Character vector paths input netCDF files. varid Character string giving name variable concatenate. missing, variable inferred first file possible. output Character string giving path output netCDF file create.

title: Concatenate records of a variable across netCDF files — nc_rcat
text: Invisibly returns `output`.

title: Concatenate records of a variable across netCDF files — nc_rcat
text: files must contain selected variable must compatible non-unlimited dimensions. unlimited dimension appended order given `filenames`.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ nc_rcat(   filenames = c("part1.nc", "part2.nc", "part3.nc"),   varid = "temp",   output = "combined.nc" ) } # }

what: Rename variables and dimensions in a netCDF file — nc_rename
title: Rename variables and dimensions in a netCDF file — nc_rename
text: Renames one variables /dimensions existing netCDF file, optionally writing result new file.

title: Rename variables and dimensions in a netCDF file — nc_rename
code: nc_rename(   filename,   oldnames,   newnames,   output,   verbose = FALSE,   overwrite = FALSE )

title: Rename variables and dimensions in a netCDF file — nc_rename
text: filename Character string giving path input netCDF file. oldnames Character vector current names variables dimensions rename. newnames Character vector replacement names. Must order length `oldnames`. output Optional character string giving path output file. omitted, `overwrite = TRUE` must used original file replaced. verbose Logical. `TRUE`, print renaming operations performed. overwrite Logical. `TRUE`, allow overwriting existing output file. Use modifying original file place.

title: Rename variables and dimensions in a netCDF file — nc_rename
text: Invisibly returns path modified file, `NULL`   invisibly nothing changed.

title: Rename variables and dimensions in a netCDF file — nc_rename
text: Names `oldnames` match either variable dimension file ignored warning. none requested names found, function exits without making changes.

title: Rename variables and dimensions in a netCDF file — nc_rename
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ nc_rename(   filename = "input.nc",   oldnames = c("lon", "lat", "temp"),   newnames = c("longitude", "latitude", "temperature"),   output = "renamed.nc" ) } # }

what: Subset a variable in a netCDF file — nc_subset
title: Subset a variable in a netCDF file — nc_subset
text: Extracts subset variable netCDF file using coordinate bounds supplied one dimensions, writes result new file.

title: Subset a variable in a netCDF file — nc_subset
code: nc_subset(   filename,   varid,   output,   newvarid,   compression,   force_v4 = FALSE,   ...,   ignore.case = FALSE,   drop = FALSE )

title: Subset a variable in a netCDF file — nc_subset
text: filename Character string giving path input netCDF file. varid Character string giving name variable subset. missing file contains single variable, variable used. output Character string giving path output netCDF file create. newvarid Optional character string giving name variable output file. default, original variable name used. compression Optional numeric compression level output variable. Supplying typically requires netCDF4 output. force_v4 Logical. `TRUE`, force creation netCDF4 output file. ... Named bounds dimensions subset. argument name must match dimension name. Values numeric vectors length two giving lower upper bounds. length-one value treated exact coordinate expanded internally `c(x, x)`. ignore.case Logical. `TRUE`, ignore case matching dimension names supplied `...`. drop Logical. `TRUE`, drop dimensions length one output.

title: Subset a variable in a netCDF file — nc_subset
text: Invisibly returns `output`, `NULL` invisibly none   supplied dimension names match variable dimensions.

title: Subset a variable in a netCDF file — nc_subset
text: dimensions named `...` subset; others kept full. output file contains subsetted variable, updated dimension values. Global attributes source file copied output file, global `history` attribute updated function call.

title: Subset a variable in a netCDF file — nc_subset
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ nc_subset(   filename = "input.nc",   varid = "temp",   output = "subset.nc",   lon = c(-80, -70),   lat = c(-20, -10),   depth = c(0, 100) ) } # }

what: Write multiple attributes to a netCDF variable or file — ncatt_put_all
title: Write multiple attributes to a netCDF variable or file — ncatt_put_all
text: Writes several attributes variable global file metadata open netCDF file.

title: Write multiple attributes to a netCDF variable or file — ncatt_put_all
code: ncatt_put_all(   nc,   varid,   attname,   attval,   prec = NA,   verbose = FALSE,   definemode = FALSE )

title: Write multiple attributes to a netCDF variable or file — ncatt_put_all
text: nc open netCDF connection created [ncdf4::nc_open()]. varid Variable identifier. can character string giving variable name, object class `ncvar4`, integer id. special case, `varid = 0` writes global attributes. attname Names attributes write. Alternatively, may named vector list attribute values `attval` omitted. attval Values attributes write. omitted, `attname` must named vector list whose names used attribute names. prec Optional precision used writing attributes. Passed [ncdf4::ncatt_put()]. verbose Logical. `TRUE`, print additional information writing attributes. definemode Logical. Passed [ncdf4::ncatt_put()]. See function details.

title: Write multiple attributes to a netCDF variable or file — ncatt_put_all
text: `NULL`, invisibly.

title: Write multiple attributes to a netCDF variable or file — ncatt_put_all
text: Attributes can supplied either parallel `attname` `attval` arguments, single named vector list. Missing attribute names allowed.

title: Write multiple attributes to a netCDF variable or file — ncatt_put_all
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ nc <- ncdf4::nc_open("input.nc", write = TRUE) on.exit(ncdf4::nc_close(nc))  ncatt_put_all(   nc,   varid = "temp",   attname = c("long_name", "units"),   attval = list("Temperature", "degree_C") )  ncatt_put_all(   nc,   varid = 0,   attval = list(title = "Example file", source = "nctools") ) } # }

title: Internal netCDF helpers — nctools-internal
text: Developer-facing helper functions used internally `nctools` variable validation, dimension matching, renaming variables dimensions netCDF files. `.replaceInDim()` replaces one component named dimension inside variable definition. `.nc_renameDim()` rewrites netCDF file one dimensions renamed. `.nc_renameVar()` renames one variables netCDF file. `.checkVarid()` validates variable identifier open netCDF file resolves default variable file contains single variable. `.getDimensions()` matches dimensions array target netCDF dimension-size vector.

title: Internal netCDF helpers — nctools-internal
code: .replaceInDim(x, dim, id, value)  .nc_renameDim(filename, oldname, newname, output, verbose = FALSE)  .nc_renameVar(filename, oldname, newname, output, verbose = FALSE)  .checkVarid(varid, nc)  .getDimensions(x, dimsize)

title: Internal netCDF helpers — nctools-internal
text: x Array-like object. dim Character string giving dimension name modify. id Character string giving component dimension object replace, example `"name"`. value Replacement value selected component. filename Character string giving path input netCDF file. oldname Character vector giving existing dimension names. newname Character vector giving replacement dimension names. Must length order `oldname`. output Character string giving path output netCDF file. verbose Logical. `TRUE`, report progress rewriting file. varid Variable identifier supplied user. can character string, object class `ncvar4`, `NA`/missing file contains single variable. nc open netCDF connection created [ncdf4::nc_open()]. dimsize Numeric vector giving target dimension sizes.

title: Internal netCDF helpers — nctools-internal
text: modified variable definition object. Invisibly returns `output`. Invisibly returns `output`. validated variable name character string. integer vector giving matched dimension positions.

title: Internal netCDF helpers — nctools-internal
text: functions part public API may change without notice. file rebuilt variable definitions `filename`, requested dimensions renamed writing output file. `output` differs `filename`, input file first copied `output`, renaming applied . Matching based dimension lengths. one candidate match exists, first match used.

title: Get variable dimensions from a netCDF file — ncvar_dim
text: `varid` `NULL`, named list one element per variable. `varid` supplied, dimensions selected variable . `value = FALSE`, dimensions returned character vectors dimension names. `value = TRUE`, dimensions returned named lists coordinate values.

what: Write data to a netCDF file — write_ncdf
title: Write data to a netCDF file — write_ncdf
text: `write_ncdf()` S3 generic writing R objects netCDF file. Methods provided writing single array-like object (`write_ncdf.default()`) list arrays (`write_ncdf.list()`).

title: Write data to a netCDF file — write_ncdf
code: write_ncdf(x, filename, ...)  # Default S3 method write_ncdf(   x,   filename,   varid,   dim,   longname,   units,   prec = "float",   missval = NA,   compression = 9,   chunksizes = NA,   verbose = FALSE,   dim.names,   dim.units,   dim.longname,   unlim = FALSE,   global = list(),   force_v4 = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'list' write_ncdf(   x,   filename,   varid,   dim,   longname,   units,   prec = "float",   missval = NA,   compression = 9,   chunksizes = NA,   verbose = FALSE,   dim.names,   dim.units,   dim.longname,   unlim = FALSE,   global = list(),   force_v4 = FALSE,   ... )

title: Write data to a netCDF file — write_ncdf
text: x Object write. Supported methods currently accept either single array-like object list array-like objects. filename Character string giving path netCDF file create. ... Additional arguments passed methods. varid Character string giving name variable create output file. dim named unnamed list defining coordinates dimension. element contains coordinate values one dimension. omitted, dimensions generated `seq_len()` dimension `x`. longname Character string giving long name output variable. Defaults `""`. units Character string giving units output variable. Defaults `""`. prec Character string giving storage precision passed [ncdf4::ncvar_def()], example `"short"`, `"integer"`, `"float"` `"double"`. missval Missing value used netCDF variable definition. compression Numeric compression level passed [ncdf4::ncvar_def()]. Compression usually requires netCDF4 output. chunksizes Optional chunk sizes compressed variables. verbose Logical. `ncdf4` report progress creating filling file? dim.names Character vector names dimensions `dim` unnamed. Ignored `dim` already names. dim.units Character vector giving units dimensions. omitted, empty strings used. dim.longname Character vector giving long names dimensions. omitted, empty strings used. unlim Character string naming unlimited dimension. Use `FALSE` unlimited dimension. global Named list global attributes add output file. `history` attribute documenting function call added automatically. force_v4 Logical. output file forced netCDF4 format?

title: Write data to a netCDF file — write_ncdf
text: Invisibly returns `filename`.

title: Write data to a netCDF file — write_ncdf
text: `write_ncdf.default()` writes single object `x` one variable new netCDF file. dimensions variable defined `dim`. `dim` unnamed, dimension names taken `dim.names`, generated automatically `"dim1"`, `"dim2"`, . Dimension units long names can supplied `dim.units` `dim.longname`. `write_ncdf.list()` writes element `x` separate variable netCDF file. `varid` omitted, variable names taken `names(x)`. Arguments `longname`, `units`, `prec` can supplied either length-one values, recycled variables, one value per variable. object `dim` defines full set dimensions available file. Individual variables may use subset dimensions, provided sizes consistent declared dimension lengths.

title: Write data to a netCDF file — write_ncdf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Single variable x <- array(rnorm(20 * 10), dim = c(20, 10))  write_ncdf(   x,   filename = "example_single.nc",   varid = "temp",   dim = list(lon = seq_len(20), lat = seq_len(10)),   longname = "Temperature",   units = "degree_C" )  ## Multiple variables x1 <- array(rnorm(20 * 10), dim = c(20, 10)) x2 <- array(rnorm(20 * 10), dim = c(20, 10))  write_ncdf(   list(temp = x1, salt = x2),   filename = "example_multi.nc",   varid = c("temp", "salt"),   dim = list(lon = seq_len(20), lat = seq_len(10)),   longname = c("Temperature", "Salinity"),   units = c("degree_C", "psu") ) } # }
